description: Shared fractional-index test vectors, asserted identically by the Java suite (backend/src/test/java/com/shopmate/domain/crdt/FractionalIndexTest.java) and the TypeScript suite (frontend/src/features/shopping-list/__tests__/fractionalIndex.test.ts). Pins the BUG-8 fix (docs/plans/section-grouping.md Phase 0 / PLAN.md) so the two language implementations can never silently diverge again.

append.note: overflow: first char already at alphabet max, extend length instead
overflow chains: still only the first char is ever inspected

between.note: BUG-8 regression case. The old algorithm padded 'b0' with its pad char 'a' to match length, producing 'b0a' == after exactly, then treated the pair as equal and appended a fresh suffix ('b0am'), which sorts AFTER 'b0a' -- violating the contract. Prod already has adjacent legacy keys in this exact shape.

tooTight.note: hi == lo + a single copy of the alphabet's minimum digit. Provably no string can sit strictly between two keys in that exact relationship under plain lexicographic comparison over any fixed alphabet -- both implementations must throw rather than silently return an out-of-range key.

bisectionNarrowingHi.description: Repeated between(lo, hi) with lo held fixed and hi replaced by each successive result -- the worst case for convergence toward one boundary. Each entry must satisfy lo < key < previousHi, and recomputing between(lo, previousHi) must reproduce it exactly.

bisectionNarrowingLo.description: Repeated between(lo, hi) with hi held fixed and lo replaced by each successive result -- the mirror-image worst case. Each entry must satisfy previousLo < key < hi, and recomputing between(previousLo, hi) must reproduce it exactly.
